data.title: Surf Air Mobility: After The Drop, Is The AI And Electrification Story Attractive?
data.description:
Surf Air Mobility Inc. (SRFM) stock has fallen nearly 35% since my last report. I consider the stock a very speculative play on AI and the electrification of aviation. The company is backed by Palantir (PLTR), and in my prior report, I also noted that a bullish stance on Surf Air Mobility because it is backed by Palantir Technologies was not justified. In this report, I discuss the company’s Q3 2025 earnings and update my stock price target.

Surf Air Mobility Leans On Commercial Opportunities In AI And Electrification

The appeal of Surf Air Mobility is definitely not their charter business. It is, however, the only part of the business that is currently profitable on an adjusted EBITDA basis. The bigger opportunities for Surf Air Mobility are technology and electrification solutions.

The technology solution is SurfOS, which consists of three components:

  1. BrokerOS for matching operations with clients for charter flights.
  2. OperatorOS to improve operational efficiency for flight equipment and crews.
  3. OwnerOS for advanced insights for private aircraft owners.

The platform is currently not generating revenues. The first revenues are expected to come in H1 2026. After the third quarter ended, the company raised $26 million in fresh capital for the commercialization of SurfOS and $74 million in convertible notes. This also highlights the dilution risks as the charter operations are not sustaining the development of technology and electrification solutions.

The third opportunity for Surf Air Mobility is the electrification of Cessna Grand Caravan EX airplanes. Certification has previously been delayed to 2027, so until then, we see no revenues from this effort and development, and certification is likely going to be costly with a risk of additional delays and levels of cash drains that may require additional funding if software and charter cash inflows are not sufficient.

Surf Air Mobility Sees EBITDA Loss Widen

Sequentially, sales rose $1.8 million to $29.2 million from charter operations. The EBITDA loss widened from $9.5 million to $9.9 million, which provides somewhat of an indication that charter profitability is not pacing with the negative cash and EBITDA generated in the other parts of the business. For the full year, the revenues are expected to be $105 million with profitable charter operations, but given the cash drains for SurfOS and electric power train development for the Cessna Grand Caravan, the company will remain loss making.

Surf Air Mobility Investment Case Leans On Electrification Of Airplanes

Absent of profits, I am using an EV/Sales valuation method to determine the stock price target based on sales estimates available on Seeking Alpha. I have run the valuation using two scenarios. In the first scenario, we use the company’s median EV/Sales, and in the second scenario, we use the transportation sector median. A case could be made that if substantial results are derived from the electric powertrain solutions, an aerospace multiple of 4.2x would be more appropriate. However, for the time being, I am taking a more conservative approach.

Based on a better capitalization, implied price targets for this year have risen to $2.53-$2.95, implying 8% downside in Scenario 1 and 8% upside in Scenario 2. For next year, the downside would be 11% in Scenario 1 and 3% upside in Scenario 2. In the year 2027, the upside would be 17%-35% and by 2028, this could be 43%-53% even with the convertible notes being converted into equity.

I don’t really have a price target for Surf Air Mobility as the real value additions are likely to be rendered post-2028, but we do note that, based on current capital raise expectations, where cash burns and debt maturities are to be carried through capital raises, the target for 2028 has come down by $1.06-$1.44. I believe that the current prices offer a better entry point as the stock is now closer valued to the fair value, and ideally another 10% drop in the stock price would driven the share price to an even more compelling entry point. Over time, I do believe that there is fundamental support for $3.20 first and $3.91 later. We continue to mark this a speculative opportunity as there is substantial risk on development timelines and capital requirements.

Conclusion: Surf Air Mobility Now Offers A Better Entry Point

Surf Air Mobility is not the stock that you should own if you are looking for near-term value. The real value is in the product launches next year and the commercial entry of the power train solutions. With negative cash flows in the years ahead, Surf Air Mobility remains a very speculative high-risk/high-reward play.
